title: Bhagavad Gita
author: Vyasa
description: A 700-verse Hindu scripture that is part of the epic Mahabharata, presenting a synthesis of the concept of Dharma, Bhakti, and Moksha.

title: Rigveda Samhita
author: Ancient Rishis
description: The oldest of the four Vedas and one of the oldest extant texts in any Indo-European language, containing 1028 hymns dedicated to various deities.

title: Isha Upanishad
author: Unknown
description: One of the shortest Upanishads embedded in the Shukla Yajurveda, focusing on the nature of the ultimate reality (Brahman) and the Self (Atman).

title: Ramayana
author: Valmiki
description: An ancient Indian epic narrating the life of Rama, prince of Ayodhya, and his quest to rescue his wife Sita from the demon king Ravana.

title: Ashtavakra Gita
author: Ashtavakra
description: A radical Advaita Vedanta text presenting a dialogue between King Janaka and sage Ashtavakra on the nature of the soul and liberation.

title: Yoga Sutras of Patanjali
author: Patanjali
description: A collection of 196 sutras on the theory and practice of yoga, forming the foundational text of classical Yoga philosophy.

title: Chanakya Niti
author: Chanakya (Kautilya)
description: A collection of aphorisms on statecraft, ethics, and wisdom attributed to the ancient Indian polymath Chanakya.

title: Shrimad Bhagavatam
author: Vyasa
description: One of the eighteen Mahapuranas focusing on devotion (bhakti) to Lord Krishna, containing stories of Vishnu's avatars and teachings.

title: Madhushala
author: Harivansh Rai Bachchan
description: A celebrated collection of 135 rubaiyat (quatrains) that uses wine and tavern as metaphors for life's journey, purpose, and existential meaning.

title: Godan
author: Munshi Premchand
description: Premchand's last complete novel depicting the struggles of a poor peasant in rural India, exploring themes of exploitation, poverty, and human dignity.

title: Rashmirathi
author: Ramdhari Singh 'Dinkar'
description: An epic Hindi poem retelling the story of Karna from the Mahabharata, portraying him as a tragic hero caught between loyalty and dharma.

title: Chandrakanta
author: Devaki Nandan Khatri
description: The first modern Hindi novel known for its romantic plot and elements of fantasy, considered a landmark in Hindi literature history.

title: Nishiddha Prem
author: Jaishankar Prasad
description: A historical romance novel set during the Gupta period, exploring themes of forbidden love, duty, and sacrifice in royal court intrigue.

title: Kabir Dohawali
author: Kabir Das
description: A compilation of couplets (doha) by the 15th-century mystic poet Kabir, blending Hindu and Sufi spiritual traditions with earthy, accessible language.

title: Maila Aanchal
author: Phanishwar Nath 'Renu'
description: A pioneering work of regional realism (Anchalik Katha) depicting village life in Bihar with vivid characters and authentic dialects.

title: Pride and Prejudice
author: Jane Austen
description: A classic novel following Elizabeth Bennet as she navigates issues of manners, upbringing, morality, marriage, and social standing in Regency England.

title: Frankenstein; or, The Modern Prometheus
author: Mary Shelley
description: The groundbreaking Gothic novel about scientist Victor Frankenstein who creates a sentient creature in an unorthodox scientific experiment.

title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
description: A collection of twelve short stories featuring the iconic detective Sherlock Holmes and Dr. Watson solving mysteries in Victorian London.

title: Moby-Dick
author: Herman Melville
description: The epic tale of Ishmael's voyage on the whaleship Pequod, captained by Ahab's obsessive hunt for the white whale Moby Dick.

title: Alice's Adventures in Wonderland
author: Lewis Carroll
description: The whimsical tale of Alice falling through a rabbit hole into a fantastical world populated by peculiar anthropomorphic creatures.

title: Dracula
author: Bram Stoker
description: A seminal gothic horror novel telling the story of Count Dracula's attempt to move from Transylvania to England and spread the undead curse.

title: The Odyssey
author: Homer
description: The ancient Greek epic poem following Odysseus' ten-year journey home after the fall of Troy, facing mythical monsters and divine obstacles.

title: Don Quixote
author: Miguel de Cervantes
description: Widely considered the first modern novel, it follows the adventures of a deluded knight-errant and his faithful squire Sancho Panza through Spain.

title: Dhammapada
author: Unknown (attributed to Buddha)
description: A collection of sayings of the Buddha in verse form, covering themes of ethics, mindfulness, suffering, and the path to enlightenment.

title: The Heart Sutra (Prajnaparamita Hridaya)
author: Unknown
description: A core Mahayana Buddhist text condensing the essence of prajna (wisdom), teaching the doctrine of emptiness (shunyata) in just a few pages.

title: The Tibetan Book of the Dead (Bardo Thodol)
author: Padmasambhava (traditionally)
description: A guide for the dead describing the experiences encountered between death and rebirth, used by Tibetan Buddhists to navigate the intermediate bardo states.
